CHEQUP
  • test_2d module
  • View page source

test_2d module

This script tests that the 2D code produces the correct Sedov-Taylor blast wave solution.

It assumes that the code has already been compiled in ../sim_folder/build/ (using 1T model)

test_2d.check_blast_radius_isotropy_t(sim_data, sol, tol_r: int = 10, tol_iso: float = 0.5)[source]

This function compute the time evolution of the blast radius aswell as its isotrpy, and verify that the error is below tol%.

test_2d.check_density_profile_r(sim_data, sol, tol: int = 21)[source]

Compare radial density profiles at several output times to the analytical solution. Raise error if the mean relative L2 error is over 15%.

test_2d.check_energy_conservation(sim_data, tol: float = 1.0)[source]
test_2d.cleanup_outputs(extra_file='')[source]
test_2d.run_castro_simulation(model='gamma_law', runtime_options='')[source]

Run the Castro simulation. Raise an error and print stdout/stderr if the command fails.

test_2d.test_2d_sedov_taylor()[source]

Test that code produce the exact Sedov-Taylor blast wave solution, in a simplified setup: - no ionization reactions (castro.add_ext_src=0) - no temperature diffusion (castro.diffuse_temp=0)
